_comment: Assassin's Creed franchise data file. Covers all main games, spin-offs, films, books, comics, and associated media.
description: Ubisoft's flagship franchise redefining historical action-adventure since 2007. Centers on the millennia-old conflict between the Assassin Brotherhood and the Templar Order, explored through the Animus—a device allowing users to relive ancestral memories. Known for its deep lore, historical tourism, and a controversial modern-day framing story.

media.title: Assassin's Creed
media.description: The game that started it all. Altaïr Ibn-La'Ahad in the Holy Land during the Third Crusade. Introduced the core mechanics and the modern-day protagonist Desmond Miles.
media.notes: Developed from Prince of Persia: Assassins. Director's Cut for PC added several new mission types.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Altaïr's Chronicles
media.description: A prequel to the first game, following Altaïr's mission to recover the Chalice. A 2.5D side-scroller and action game.
media.notes: Mobile spin-off. Story is partially contradicted by later canon but was considered canon at release. Removed from modern app stores.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Bloodlines
media.description: Direct sequel to the first game, continuing Altaïr's story in Cyprus as he pursues the Templars and meets Maria Thorpe.
media.notes: PSP exclusive. Considered canon, bridging the gap between AC1 and the Altaïr sections of AC2. Emulatable via PPSSPP.

media.title: Assassin's Creed II
media.description: Follows Ezio Auditore da Firenze during the Italian Renaissance. A massive leap forward in gameplay, story, and scope, widely considered a masterpiece.
media.notes: GOTY edition includes the Battle of Forli and Bonfire of the Vanities DLC. Ezio's trilogy begins here.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Discovery
media.description: A 2.5D platformer set during the events of ACII, following Ezio as he aids Christopher Columbus's voyage to the New World.
media.notes: Canonical events referenced in ACII: Discovery. The DS and iOS versions differ significantly. Removed from modern app stores.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Project Legacy
media.description: A text-based RPG on Facebook. Players completed missions and unlocked memories of various Assassins. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Facebook game. Served as a companion app for AC: Brotherhood. Shut down in 2013. Contained crucial lore for characters like Giovanni Borgia.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood
media.description: Direct sequel to ACII. Ezio travels to Rome to rebuild the Assassin Brotherhood and defeat the Borgia family. Introduced the Brotherhood management system and multiplayer.
media.notes: The first to feature a full multiplayer mode. Da Vinci Disappearance DLC is canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Revelations
media.description: Concludes Ezio's trilogy and Altaïr's story. An aging Ezio travels to Constantinople to find the keys to Altaïr's library.
media.notes: Features the Mediterranean Defense mobile game-like mechanic. Lost Archive DLC reveals more of Subject 16's (Clay Kaczmarek) backstory.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Multiplayer Rearmed
media.description: A standalone mobile adaptation of the multiplayer mode from Brotherhood and Revelations. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Removed from the App Store after iOS 11. No longer playable.

media.title: Assassin's Creed III
media.description: Concludes Desmond's saga. Features Connor (Ratonhnhaké:ton) during the American Revolution. Overhauled the engine, introducing naval combat and tree-running.
media.notes: The Remastered version (included with AC Odyssey Season Pass) features improved visuals and mechanics.

media.title: Assassin's Creed III: Liberation
media.description: Spin-off set in New Orleans during the French and Indian War. Features Aveline de Grandpré, the franchise's first female protagonist.
media.notes: Originally a Vita exclusive. HD remaster included in ACIII Remastered. Canon with a minor crossover with ACIII.

media.title: Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag
media.description: Pirate Edward Kenway, father of Haytham and grandfather of Connor, in the Golden Age of Piracy. Expanded naval gameplay and is widely considered a masterpiece of the series.
media.notes: Introduced modern-day protagonist 'Noob' (an employee of Abstergo Entertainment). A classic often cited as the best pirate game ever made.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Pirates
media.description: Naval combat and exploration mobile game featuring Alonzo Batilla, a contemporary of Edward Kenway. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Removed from app stores in 2017 after servers were shut down. The story is non-canonical but featured appearances by canon characters like Blackbeard and Adewale.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Memories
media.description: A collectible card game (CCG) that covered battles across various historical periods. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Free-to-play card game. Servers shut down in 2016. Removed from app stores.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Identity
media.description: A 3D action-RPG set during the Italian Renaissance. Players create an Assassin and complete missions in a semi-open-world Florence and Rome.
media.notes: Originally released in Australia in 2014, globally in 2016. Removed from app stores in 2022. Considered non-canon due to the custom character and timeline contradictions.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Unity
media.description: Arno Dorian during the French Revolution. The first truly 'next-gen' AC, featuring dense crowds, co-op multiplayer, and a massive recreation of Paris.
media.notes: Notorious for its buggy launch, but since patched. The companion app (AC Unity Companion) is DEFUNCT.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Rogue
media.description: Shay Patrick Cormac, an Assassin who becomes a Templar, hunting his former brothers during the Seven Years' War. Connects the stories of Black Flag, Unity, and ACIII.
media.notes: Released on the same day as Unity. A swan song for the previous console generation with refined naval combat from Black Flag.

media.title: Assassin's Creed Chronicles: China
media.description: A 2.5D stealth-platformer. Follows Shao Jun, the last Chinese Assassin, after the events of the Assassin's Creed: Embers short film.
media.notes: The first of the Chronicles trilogy, set in 16th-century China during the Ming dynasty. Features beautiful ink-wash art style.

media.title: Assassin's Creed Chronicles: India
media.description: Second Chronicles game. Features Arbaaz Mir in 1841 Amritsar during the Sikh Empire, tasked with retrieving an Apple of Eden from the Templars.
media.notes: Set during the Anglo-Sikh wars. Connects to the Brahman comic series.

media.title: Assassin's Creed Chronicles: Russia
media.description: Final Chronicles game. Nikolai Orelov in 1918 Russia during the Bolshevik Revolution. Features the modern-day protagonist Anastasia Nikolaevna.
media.notes: Connects the Fall and The Chain comic series directly into the game canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Syndicate
media.description: Jacob and Evie Frye in Victorian London. Focused on gang warfare and liberating districts from Templar control. Introduced a grappling hook and vehicle combat.
media.notes: Ubisoft Quebec's first full AC title. The Jack the Ripper DLC is a notable expansion.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Origins
media.description: A soft reboot. Set in Ptolemaic Egypt, it tells the story of Bayek of Siwa, one of the founding members of the Hidden Ones, the precursor to the Assassin Brotherhood.
media.notes: Shifted the series to an action-RPG with loot, levels, and skill trees. Introduced Discovery Tour, an educational museum mode.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Odyssey
media.description: Set in ancient Greece during the Peloponnesian War. Play as Alexios or Kassandra, a mercenary who becomes a key figure in the fight to control ancient secrets.
media.notes: Expanded the RPG elements with dialogue choices and branching quests. Features naval combat and is set centuries before the formation of the Hidden Ones.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Valhalla
media.description: Eivor Varinsdottir, a Viking raider, settles in 9th-century England. Blends the RPG formula with a return to classic Assassin vs. Templar (Order of the Ancients) conflict.
media.notes: The most content-rich AC game to date with multiple expansions. Ties together the lore of the Isu and the modern-day narrative.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Mirage
media.description: A return to the series' roots. Set in 9th-century Baghdad, it follows Basim Ibn Ishaq from Valhalla in his origin story from street thief to master Assassin.
media.notes: Celebrates the 15th anniversary of the franchise. Focuses on stealth, parkour, and a dense urban environment.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Shadows
media.description: Set in Feudal Japan. Features two protagonists: Naoe, a shinobi Assassin, and Yasuke, a legendary samurai based on the historical figure. Released in March 2026.
media.notes: The most recent entry in the series. Marks the first AC game set in East Asia on a modern scale.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Nexus VR
media.description: A first-person virtual reality experience. Features three distinct campaigns playing as Ezio (Renaissance Italy), Kassandra (Ancient Greece), and Connor (American Revolution).
media.notes: Exclusive to Meta Quest platforms. A full-fledged AC game built from the ground up for VR.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Codename Jade
media.description: An open-world AAA mobile game set in ancient China (the Qin and Han dynasties) during the Qin dynasty's conquest for unification. Features a custom protagonist.
media.notes: Also known as 'Assassin's Creed: Jade' in some marketing. Released globally in early 2024. Its canonicity is currently ambiguous.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Rebellion
media.description: A base-building and RPG-like strategy game. Players collect and train over 100 Assassins from across the franchise to battle the Templar order in Spain during the Spanish Inquisition.
media.notes: Features characters from ACII, Brotherhood, Black Flag, and more. Still available for download as of 2026. The story is considered non-canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed (Animated Web Series)
media.description: A promotional animated short series created by Ubisoft. Adapted moments from the first game and bridging content between AC1 and AC2.
media.notes: Short episodes: 'Desmond' (3 min), 'Abstergo' (3 min), 'Assassins' (3 min). Considered part of the extended lore.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Embers
media.description: An animated short film included with the Assassin's Creed: Revelations Signature Edition. It depicts the final days of Ezio Auditore and the training of Shao Jun.
media.notes: 21 minutes long. A poignant and essential conclusion to the Ezio trilogy.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Lineage
media.description: A series of live-action short films (three parts) that act as a prequel to Assassin's Creed II, focusing on Ezio's father, Giovanni Auditore.
media.notes: 35 minutes total. Directed by Yves Simoneau. Considered canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Ascendance
media.description: A 15-minute animated short film bridging the gap between Assassin's Creed II and Brotherhood, focusing on Cesare Borgia's rise to power.
media.notes: Part of the Brotherhood marketing campaign. Considered canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed (Theatrical Film)
media.description: Live-action film starring Michael Fassbender as Callum Lynch and his ancestor Aguilar de Nerha during the Spanish Inquisition. Released by 20th Century Fox.
media.notes: Set in the same universe as the games but follows an original story. The film was a box office success but received mixed reviews. The story is considered canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: The Fall
media.description: A 3-issue comic miniseries by WildStorm (DC Comics) that follows Nikolai Orelov in 20th-century Russia and his descendant Daniel Cross in the modern day.
media.notes: Crucial modern-day lore. Introduces Daniel Cross, a key character in ACIII's modern-day storyline. Written by Cameron Stewart and Karl Kerschl.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: The Chain
media.description: The direct sequel to The Fall, continuing Nikolai Orelov's story as he escapes Russia with the Shroud of Eden.
media.notes: A single 48-page comic. The events tie directly into the Assassin's Creed Chronicles: Russia game.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Brahman
media.description: A 3-issue comic set in 19th-century India during the Sikh Empire. Follows Arbaaz Mir and introduces the modern-day protagonist Monima Das.
media.notes: Ties into the Assassin's Creed Chronicles: India game and features the Koh-i-Noor artifact. Written by Cameron Stewart.

media.title: Assassin's Creed (Titan Comics Series)
media.description: A 14-issue comic series by Titan Comics, set in London during the Industrial Revolution. Features Charlotte de la Cruz, a modern-day Assassin.
media.notes: Ongoing series (Vol. 1: Trial by Fire, Vol. 2: Setting Sun, Vol. 3: Homecoming). Considered canon. Follows the Phoenix Project arc from the games.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Uprising
media.description: A 12-issue sequel to the Titan Comics series, concluding the modern-day Juno storyline that began in the games and comics.
media.notes: Volumes: 'The Inflection Point', 'The Fire Trial', 'The Finale'. Essential reading for fans of the modern-day lore.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Bloodstone
media.description: A 2-volume French comic (later translated) set in Vietnam during the Cold War. Follows the Assassin Tomo Sakagawa and her dealings with a resurrected templar.
media.notes: Part of the 'Les Deux Royaumes' cycle, connected to the French comics. Art by Salem Zambrano.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Valhalla — Song of Glory
media.description: A 3-issue prequel comic to Valhalla, detailing Eivor's early raids and her relationship with the Norse king, Harald Finehair.
media.notes: Written by Cavan Scott. Provides essential backstory for the game's protagonist.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: The Secret Crusade
media.description: A novel by Oliver Bowden. It serves as a novelization of Assassin's Creed I, Bloodlines, and reveals Altaïr's entire life, including his final days.
media.notes: Part of the official novel series published by Penguin Books. Considered canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Renaissance
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novelization of Assassin's Creed II.
media.notes: Canon. The first of the Ezio trilogy of novels.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novelization of Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Revelations
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novelization of Assassin's Creed: Revelations.
media.notes: Canon.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Forsaken
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novel that serves as a prequel to Assassin's Creed III, told from the perspective of Haytham Kenway.
media.notes: Essential reading for the Kenway saga. Provides a deeper look into Haytham's motivations.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novelization of Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag.
media.notes: Canon. Includes some additional scenes not present in the game.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Unity
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novelization of Assassin's Creed: Unity.
media.notes: Canon. Features a framing story with the modern-day protagonist from the game.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Underworld
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novel that serves as a prequel to Assassin's Creed: Syndicate, focusing on Henry Green's time in India and London.
media.notes: Essential backstory for the Frye twins' ally.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Desert Oath
media.description: Oliver Bowden's novel that serves as a prequel to Assassin's Creed: Origins, following Bayek's youth and his father's murder.
media.notes: Written by Oliver Bowden (Anton Gill). Provides crucial backstory for the game's protagonist.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Odyssey
media.description: A novelization of Assassin's Creed: Odyssey by Gordon Doherty.
media.notes: Officially licensed novel published by Ubisoft. Focuses on Kassandra as the canon protagonist.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Valhalla — Geirmund's Saga
media.description: A novel by Matthew J. Kirby. Follows the story of Geirmund Hel-hide, a jarl who becomes a key figure in Eivor's story in England.
media.notes: A tie-in novel to Valhalla. The main character appears in the game's River Raids update.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: The Ming Storm
media.description: A Chinese webnovel and eventual print series by Yan Lianke. Follows Shao Jun after the events of Chronicles: China.
media.notes: Published in China. Considered an extension of the Shao Jun storyline.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Gold (Audible Original)
media.description: A full-cast audio drama produced by Audible. Set in the 19th-century California Gold Rush, following two rival Assassins.
media.notes: Voice cast includes Riz Ahmed and Anthony Head. A fully produced audio drama, not just a book reading.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: The First Blade
media.description: A full-cast audio drama by Audible. A prequel to the Odyssey DLC, set in Persia and following the first wielder of the Hidden Blade.
media.notes: Starring a full cast, including Anthony Head. An original story expanding on the lore of Darius.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: The Board Game
media.description: A Kickstarter-funded board game. A narrative-driven, campaign-based game where players act as Renaissance Italian Assassins. A digital version (Tabletopia) also exists.
media.notes: Standalone game with modular board. Not to be confused with the earlier 'Arena' board game or the 'Brotherhood of Venice' expansion.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Arena
media.description: A competitive board game for 2-4 players. Players control an Assassin in a gladiatorial arena trying to eliminate others. Out of print.
media.notes: Based on the multiplayer mode of Brotherhood and Revelations.

media.title: Assassin's Creed: Vendetta
media.description: A dice and card game based on the film. Players hunt Templars across Renaissance Spain.
media.notes: A tie-in for the 2016 movie. Out of print.
